query: Angular standalone migration AppModule bootstrapApplication route providers

selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No single-topic GreatFrontEnd page was confirmed for this exact Angular prompt; the available Angular basics coverage is broader than the question-specific comparison needed here.

competitors.label: Angular Standalone Migration Guide
competitors.selectionReason: The official standalone migration guide is the canonical source for converting declarations, bootstrapping, and module imports during migration.
theirStrengths: It gives the official migration sequence, code transformations, and safety steps for moving from AppModule-era wiring.
theirGaps: It is migration-instruction heavy and less concise about the interview-style mapping of each AppModule responsibility to its new standalone home.

realWorldUseCases: The main pitfall is accidentally changing DI scope while you migrate.
Now checkout gets a new CartStore instance. The navbar outside that route still sees the root instance.
followUpConfusion: Global bootstrapping config belongs in bootstrapApplication / app.config.ts. Per-component template scope belongs in each standalone component’s imports. Route-level providers are for intentionally scoped feature services.

competitors.winReason: The migration guide is still the canonical runbook, but our page now wins on the exact interview-friendly mapping: what moved where, which DI scope bugs show up, and how to keep bootstrap, template scope, and route providers separate.

realWorldUseCases: The Angular migration guide is operationally grounded, so the use-case framing stays close.
actionableExamples: It provides the strongest concrete migration steps and code transforms in this set.
followUpConfusion: It explains standalone conversion mechanics well, but it is less compact than our page on the exact “what moved where” decision tree.

actionableExamples: Keep the before-and-after bootstrap example plus the moved-from / moved-to map together so the page stays easier to execute than the full migration guide.

competitors.label: Angular University Standalone Components Complete Guide
competitors.selectionReason: This guide is a strong migration-oriented tutorial competitor because it covers standalone bootstrapping, interoperability, and lazy-loading changes.
theirStrengths: It explains the standalone shift, bootstrapping, imports, and interoperability in a detailed migration narrative.
theirGaps: It is broader than our AppModule-specific prompt and spends more time on why standalone matters overall.

realWorldUseCases: The main pitfall is accidentally changing DI scope while you migrate.
followUpConfusion: If a service should stay app-wide, do not move it to a route just because the old AppModule is disappearing.

competitors.winReason: Angular University gives a broader standalone narrative, but our page is now tighter on the exact migration follow-ups interviewers ask: what moved out of AppModule, where provider scope changes, and which responsibilities stay global.

realWorldUseCases: Angular University stays practical about migration and interoperability, so the use-case comparison is close.
actionableExamples: It includes enough standalone code examples that the example comparison remains close.
followUpConfusion: Its broader migration walkthrough gives more surrounding context on interoperability, lazy loading, and bootstrap changes than our shorter trivia page.

actionableExamples: If this stays close, add one more concrete example of importing a leftover module-shaped library through importProvidersFrom during migration.

competitors.label: Telerik Standalone and Modules Combined
competitors.selectionReason: This article is a practical competitor because it explains app.config.ts, provideRouter, component imports, and coexistence with module-based packages.
theirStrengths: It directly discusses what replaced app.module.ts and how standalone apps wire routing and imports.
theirGaps: It is more about coexistence than about the sharper migration-map answer our page gives for each AppModule responsibility.

realWorldUseCases: The main pitfall is accidentally changing DI scope while you migrate.
actionableExamples: Moved-from / moved-to map for the AppModule era
Before: one app-wide store via AppModule

competitors.winReason: Telerik explains the new standalone structure well, but our page now wins on the concrete migration artifacts interviewers probe for: a moved-from / moved-to map and a provider-scope regression example.

realWorldUseCases: Telerik shows the new structure well, but our page is more directly organized around the DI-scope mistakes teams hit during migration.
actionableExamples: Its app.config.ts and component-import examples make the example criterion close.
followUpConfusion: It explains provideRouter and imports clearly, but it is less explicit about keeping the NgModules-vs-standalone question separate from the AppModule migration question.
Keep the explicit note that AppModule replacement, NgModule compatibility, and route-level provider scope are three separate decisions.
